title: rubus × assurgens var. parvulus
intro: Rubus × assurgens var. parvulus, also known as the dwarf blackberry, is a hybrid species of the Rubus genus, characterized by its compact growth and small, edible fruits. The leaves and roots of Rubus × assurgens var. parvulus have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and skin irritations. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and acting as an antiseptic agent due to its rich content of antioxidants and tannins. Rubus × assurgens var. parvulus can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its leaves and roots can also be used in salves and ointments to support skin health and wound recovery.
